self_report.reflection: Using t+1/t = (\sqrt{t}-1/\sqrt{t})^2 + 2 and the symmetrization t\mapsto 1/t with u=\sqrt{t}-1/\sqrt{t}, one obtains I = e^{-2a}\sqrt{\pi/a}. The claim's exponential factor e^{-a} is missing the factor of 2 from the '+2' constant in completing the square.
self_report.notes: Standard Putnam 1985 B-5 result is \sqrt{\pi/a} e^{-2a}, not e^{-a}.
